response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has a broad software engineering background with genuine craftsmanship values, but her experience is almost entirely outside the JVM ecosystem. Her primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang; Java appears only marginally in older roles (robotic arms, mobile orchestration at Altom) without any indication of JVM depth. There is no evidence of Spring, Kotlin, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise Java framework usage. The role requires deep JVM expertise, IDE internals knowledge, and enterprise framework experience — none of which this candidate demonstrates at a meaningful level. Her most recent roles at re:cinq and Optilyz are squarely in TypeScript/Node.js territory.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple senior roles
Experience building and operating high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second), demonstrating ability to work on complex, large-scale codebases
Developer tooling background including MCP code generators and IDE-adjacent C/C++ component development at National Instruments
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth — Java appears in the skills list but only briefly surfaces in non-JVM-focused contexts in older roles
Zero Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework experience anywhere in the resume
No JVM-based IDE internals or language tooling experience — the NI role involved a C IDE (LabWindows/CVI), not JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: Strong craftsmanship and complex systems track record
